title: Blackstone Redemptions Test Liquidity As Shares Trade Below Fair Value
text: Blackstone's flagship private credit fund, BCRED, has seen a sharp rise in investor redemption requests. In response, Blackstone raised the fund's repurchase cap and injected additional capital to meet all redemption demands. The decision to fully honor requests and support the fund with fresh capital marks an unusual move in the private credit market. For investors watching NYSE:BX, this comes at a time when the share price is around $114.14 and recent stock performance has been weak, with...

title: Is Blackstone (BX) Now At A Reasonable Price After Recent Share Price Slide
text: If you are wondering whether Blackstone's current share price reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article is going to focus squarely on what you might be paying for each dollar of value in the business. Blackstone's share price closed at US$114.14, with a 3.5% decline over 7 days, a 19.2% decline over 30 days, a 28.1% decline year to date and a 22.4% decline over 1 year, set against longer term returns of 36.9% over 3 years and 91.3% over 5 years. Recent headlines around...

title: Blue Owl Stock Has Plunged on Private Credit Fears. This Analyst Says They’re Overblown.
text: Wall Street believes  Blue Owl Capital  has a major credit problem.  Chris Kotowski at Oppenheimer disagrees.  “We do not believe in the narrative of a broad-based deterioration in private credit,” Kotowski declared in a research report Wednesday.

title: KKR Co-CEOs Keep Buying the Stock Dip
text: Two top executives at  KKR  keep buying the dip in the firm’s beleaguered stock.  Co-CEOs Joseph Bae and Scott Nuttall purchased a combined $8.8 million worth of KKR common stock last week, according to regulatory filings.  Independent board director Mary Dillon bought $2 million worth of KKR stock on Monday.

title: KKR Insider Buying And New Retail Fund Reshape Growth Story
text: KKR (NYSE:KKR) Co CEOs and an independent board director recently made significant insider purchases of KKR shares, increasing their personal stakes in the firm. The company has also launched its first private equity fund for individual investors in partnership with Capital Group, expanding access beyond traditional institutional clients. title: How The Story Is Shifting For KKR (KKR) As Analysts Rework Valuation Targets
text: The average fair value estimate for KKR has been revised from US$143.87 to US$140.40 per share, signaling a modest reset in where analysts currently see equilibrium value. That adjustment sits alongside a wave of fresh initiations, rating changes, and target cuts to levels like US$127, US$125 and US$168, as well as at least one move to Hold, showing how split the Street is between long term franchise views and nearer term execution risk. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets...

title: Washed-Out Private-Credit Stocks Approach Technical Inflection Points
text: Publicly traded lenders and alternative asset managers have come under pressure amid concerns over credit quality and liquidity.  KKR & Co  is clearly struggling, down 27% so far this year.  The stock is now 39% off its most recent 52-week high but is attempting to end a seven-week losing streak.

title: Private Market Titans Warn of Pain as Credit Cracks Widen
text: From Blue Owl Capital Inc. to Blackstone Inc., private credit funds across the industry are facing a wave of withdrawals and analysts are warning default rates could soar if AI disrupts corporate America as much as some experts expect.  “People made choices: If you wanted a higher dividend, you could take more risk,” Apollo Global Management Inc. Chief Executive Officer Marc Rowan said on stage at the Bloomberg Invest conference.
